description_long: This is a medium-sized, octagonal wooden bucket or barrel with vertical wooden staves and two prominent dark metal bands encircling its upper and lower sections for structural reinforcement. The wood has a natural, slightly weathered appearance, with visible grain patterns and some minor discoloration or stains on certain panels. The top rim is uneven, with each stave extending slightly above the band, giving the opening a jagged, rustic look. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with traditional barrels or planters. There are no visible handles or lids, and the object is open at the top. The overall construction suggests durability and a utilitarian design suitable for holding liquids, soil, or miscellaneous items in an urban or garden setting.
description: A rustic, octagonal wooden barrel or bucket with dark metal bands and an open, uneven rim. The wood is natural and slightly weathered, with visible grain and some stains.
description_view_0: This view shows the front face of the bucket, highlighting the clean wood grain and the prominent upper and lower metal bands.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a dark stain on the upper right stave and a clear view of the metal banding, with the wood grain still visible.
description_view_2: Here, the bucket's side is shown with a small dark mark on the upper left, and the wood appears slightly lighter in tone.
description_view_3: This view displays a larger stain on the lower right stave and emphasizes the unevenness of the rim and the texture of the wood.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
